questions.scenario: Fictional company Northbridge Retail says new hires get account access in under 2 hours. As auditor, you need evidence that access provisioning is controlled.
options: Signed access request tickets approved by line managers for the last 3 months
A marketing slide saying onboarding is fast
A list of all employees with no approval trail
IAM workflow screenshots showing approval gates

feedback.0: Correct. Approved access request records prove formal authorization.
feedback.1: Trap. Marketing material does not prove control operation.
feedback.2: Trap. User lists alone do not show who approved access.
feedback.3: Correct. Workflow evidence supports design and operation of the process.

questions.scenario: Fictional fintech OrbitPay states privileged access is tightly managed.
options: PAM checkout logs for admin accounts
Developer opinion that admins are trustworthy
Role matrix mapping privileged roles to job functions
Random screenshot of a server console

feedback.0: Correct. PAM logs demonstrate actual privileged use and accountability.
feedback.1: Trap. Personal opinion is not auditable evidence.
feedback.2: Correct. Role mapping supports least privilege design.
feedback.3: Trap. Uncontextualized screenshots are weak evidence.

questions.scenario: Fictional SaaS provider HelioDesk reports it revokes access immediately after terminations.
options: HR termination list matched with account disable timestamps
Policy document with no implementation records
Ticket evidence of offboarding tasks completion
An email saying 'trust us, it is immediate'

feedback.0: Correct. Correlated HR and IAM data validates timely removal.
feedback.1: Trap. Policy alone does not prove the control operated.
feedback.2: Correct. Offboarding tickets provide operational traceability.
feedback.3: Trap. Informal assurances are not sufficient evidence.

questions.scenario: Fictional logistics firm TerraFreight claims it performs quarterly risk assessments.
options: Latest risk register with owners and treatment status
Screenshot of a calendar invite named 'Risk Meeting'
Risk methodology document approved by management
A spreadsheet with risks but no dates

feedback.0: Correct. A current risk register evidences recurring assessment output.
feedback.1: Trap. A meeting invite does not show risk analysis quality.
feedback.2: Correct. Methodology approval demonstrates defined approach.
feedback.3: Trap. Missing dates weaken reliability and recency.

questions.scenario: Fictional health app MedNova says all high risks have treatment plans.
options: Risk treatment plan linked to high-risk entries
Board minutes approving residual risk acceptance
A generic template with blank fields
Verbal statement from CTO

feedback.0: Correct. Linked treatment plans show actions for identified risks.
feedback.1: Correct. Formal acceptance records are valid when treatment is not feasible.
feedback.2: Trap. Empty templates are not evidence of operation.

questions.scenario: Fictional manufacturer BlueForge says risks are reassessed after major changes.
options: Change records with attached risk reassessment
Old risk report from last year only
Post-project review notes including risk update decisions
A poster saying 'Security First'

feedback.0: Correct. This ties change management to risk updates.
feedback.1: Trap. Outdated reports do not prove ongoing reassessment.
feedback.2: Correct. Review notes can verify decisions and actions.
feedback.3: Trap. Awareness artifacts are not control evidence.

questions.scenario: Fictional media company BrightWave says all incidents are logged and categorized.
options: Incident register with severity and closure dates
Screenshot of team chat discussing an outage
Incident handling procedure
A personal notebook of one analyst

feedback.0: Correct. Register shows execution and completeness.
feedback.1: Trap. Chat snippets are incomplete and unaudited.
feedback.2: Correct. Procedure defines expected handling approach.
feedback.3: Trap. Private notes are not controlled evidence.

questions.scenario: Fictional insurer HarborShield claims incidents are triaged within SLA.
options: Ticket timestamps showing detection-to-triage times
SLA dashboard export for incident queue
A statement: 'we always triage quickly'
An empty incident template

feedback.0: Correct. Timestamps directly test SLA compliance.
feedback.1: Correct. Dashboards can summarize performance trends.
feedback.2: Trap. Unsupported statements are weak evidence.
feedback.3: Trap. Blank templates do not show operation.

questions.scenario: Fictional cloud startup AeroStack says lessons learned are captured after incidents.
options: Post-incident reports with root cause and actions
Repeated incidents with no remediation tracking
Evidence of corrective-action tickets created from PIR outcomes
A team photo from the retrospective

feedback.0: Correct. PIR reports prove structured learning.
feedback.1: Trap. This indicates ineffectiveness, not evidence of control.
feedback.3: Trap. Photos do not evidence outcomes.

questions.scenario: Fictional e-commerce platform PixelCart says critical systems are centrally logged.
options: SIEM ingestion report for critical assets
One local server log file with no retention details
Logging standard defining what events must be captured
Engineer comment that logs are probably enabled

feedback.0: Correct. Ingestion reports validate scope of log collection.
feedback.1: Trap. Single host logs cannot prove centralized logging.
feedback.2: Correct. Standard supports control design expectations.
feedback.3: Trap. Informal comments are not evidence.

questions.scenario: Fictional biotech firm GeneTrail claims suspicious activity alerts are reviewed daily.
options: Alert review queue with analyst assignments and timestamps
A screenshot of one high-severity alert only
SOC runbook for alert handling
Noisy alert count with no review records

feedback.0: Correct. Queue history evidences ongoing monitoring operations.
feedback.1: Trap. Single examples cannot prove routine process execution.
feedback.2: Correct. Runbook supports consistency of response steps.
feedback.3: Trap. Alert volumes alone do not prove review.

questions.scenario: Fictional enterprise NovaLedger says system clocks are synchronized for log integrity.
options: NTP configuration evidence from representative systems
Monitoring report showing drift exceptions closed
A network diagram from three years ago
Analyst memory of setup during migration

feedback.0: Correct. Configuration evidence supports design implementation.
feedback.1: Correct. Closed drift alerts show ongoing maintenance.
feedback.2: Trap. Old diagrams are not current control evidence.

questions.scenario: Fictional analytics provider SkyMetric says cloud shared-responsibility is clearly managed.
options: Cloud responsibility matrix signed by security and platform teams
Cloud provider marketing brochure
Internal policy section assigning control owners
A random screenshot of cloud console home page

feedback.0: Correct. Responsibility matrix clarifies control ownership boundaries.
feedback.1: Trap. Vendor brochures are not organization-specific evidence.
feedback.2: Correct. Policy ownership mapping supports governance.
feedback.3: Trap. Generic screenshots lack control context.

questions.scenario: Fictional travel app RoutePilot claims cloud configuration baselines are enforced.
options: IaC repository showing approved secure baseline modules
Exception register for baseline deviations with risk approvals
A screenshot of one secure VM setting
An unversioned checklist with no owner

feedback.0: Correct. IaC proves repeatable baseline implementation.
feedback.1: Correct. Exception governance demonstrates managed deviations.
feedback.3: Trap. Uncontrolled documents are weak evidence.

questions.scenario: Fictional collaboration suite TeamHarbor says sensitive cloud data is protected with encryption.
options: KMS key policy and rotation evidence
Storage encryption settings export for production accounts
Statement that 'cloud defaults are secure'
A cybersecurity awareness poster

feedback.0: Correct. Key management artifacts are strong evidence.
feedback.1: Correct. Config exports prove encryption status at scale.
feedback.2: Trap. Assumptions about defaults are unreliable.
feedback.3: Trap. Posters do not prove encryption controls.
